baseline.reason: Correctly identifies the NULL sentinel source (line 622) and dereference sink (line 868), but incorrectly attributes the crash to binary-search-failure rather than dynrelcount==0. The baseline speculates that if binary search doesn't find a match, p could point to the NULL element — this is wrong because with dynrelcount>0, dynrelbuf[0] is always a valid pointer. Misses the actual fix (changing < 0 to <= 0 at line 672).

vastdb.reason: Correctly identifies that when dynrelcount==0 (no relocation entries), dynrelbuf[0] is the NULL sentinel, causing the null pointer dereference at p->howto. Accurate trigger path trace through the code. No inaccurate speculation. Minor deduction for not explicitly naming the < vs <= fix, but the root cause analysis is precise and complete.
